title: Air-fryer fish cakes
subtitle: Crisp-edged British fish cakes without shallow-frying
excerpt: Salmon and mashed potato fish cakes coated in flour and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 12 to 15 minutes. The air fryer produces a crisp outer crust without the oil required by shallow-frying, and the patties hold their shape better in the basket than they do in a hot pan.
sourceNotes: British fish cakes are made with flaked white fish or salmon mixed with mashed potato and formed into patties. The dish is a practical way of stretching a small amount of fish into a full meal and has been part of British cooking since at least the Victorian era. Thai fish cakes are a separate product made without potato and are not the same dish.

content.text: Use floury potatoes (Maris Piper) rather than waxy ones. Floury potatoes mash to a smooth paste that binds the fish cake together; waxy potatoes produce a granular, slightly sticky mash that does not hold its shape as well during cooking.

items.name: Potato
items.prepNote: Maris Piper, boiled and mashed smooth

content.text: Combine the mashed {{potato}}, flaked {{salmon-fillet}}, {{spring-onion}}, {{parsley-flat}}, {{eggs}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Mix until evenly combined. Divide into 8 equal portions and shape each into a patty about 2 cm thick. Dust all over with {{plain-flour}}, shaking off the excess. Refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Spray the basket with oil. Place the fish cakes in a single layer. Spray the tops with oil. Cook at 200°C for 12 minutes, turn carefully with a spatula, spray the second side, and cook for a further 3 minutes until deep golden on both sides.

items.fix: Chill for the full 30 minutes before cooking. The ratio should be roughly 2 parts potato to 1 part fish by weight. If the mixture is soft and difficult to shape, add a tablespoon more flour before chilling
items.cause: Not chilled long enough, or too much fish relative to potato
items.symptom: Fish cakes falling apart when flipped

items.fix: Spray both sides generously before cooking. Ensure the mashed potato was properly drained and not watery; wet mash produces a soft cake that steams rather than crisps
items.cause: Insufficient oil spray, or moisture in the mix

items.fix: Keep patties to 2 cm thick. Very cold patties from the fridge take longer; allow them to sit at room temperature for 10 minutes before cooking
items.cause: Fish cakes too thick or too cold when placed in the air fryer
items.symptom: Cold centre after 15 minutes of cooking

content.text: Air-fryer fish cakes are a classic British dinner. Serve with a dressed green salad, peas, and tartare sauce or a poached egg on top. They reheat well in the air fryer at 180°C for 6 minutes from the fridge, making them a reliable meal-prep option for the week.
